summary: Checked 27 claims, found 1 mirage (minor name mismatch on the Shift+S hotkey reference). All files, functions, structs, fields, signatures, dependencies and referenced tests in the tech-spec are confirmed against the actual code.

findings.claim: tech-spec 'How it works' (point 4) and Solution narrative say switchViewToProcPidStat is triggered by 'Shift+S' (e.g. '(`switchViewTo` -> `viewSwitchHandler`, or `Shift+S` -> `switchViewToProcPidStat`)')
findings.reality: top/keybindings.go:55 registers switchViewToProcPidStat on the plain uppercase rune 'S' (`{"sysstat", 'S', switchViewToProcPidStat(app)}`), not a Shift+S modifier combo. Per the code-research doc itself (section 9), termbox v1.1.1 cannot distinguish Shift+arrow/Shift+rune from a plain rune and gocui only exposes ModNone/ModAlt — so the binding is literally rune 'S'. Functionally a user produces 'S' by pressing Shift+s, but the tech-spec's framing as a distinct 'Shift+S' modifier path is technically inaccurate.
findings.source: 009-feat-horizontal-scroll-tech-spec.md, section Architecture / How it works (point 4) and Decision 3 narrative
findings.fix: Refer to the hotkey as 'S' (uppercase rune) to match keybindings.go:55, or note it is the rune 'S' produced via Shift; the offset-reset point in switchViewToProcPidStat remains correct regardless. No code-change impact — the two-path reset logic the spec prescribes is valid.
